Make run-java-benchmarks.sh the single source of truth for JMH settings (#153)

* Make run-java-benchmarks.sh the single source of truth for JMH settings

Remove @Fork, @Warmup, @Measurement annotations from all benchmark classes
so there are no hidden overrides. The script now controls all settings:

- Default mode: publication-quality (5 forks, 5×10s warmup, 5×10s measurement)
- --quick flag: dev iteration (1 fork, 3×1s warmup, 5×1s measurement)
- Pathological benchmarks: automatically use -f 0 (JDK can hang)

This eliminates the contradiction where AGENTS.md said 'don't set JMH_OPTS'
but annotations produced quick-iteration-quality results (1 fork, 1s iters).
Now the default run is always safe for publication.

Fixes #152

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add --smoke mode for CI; fix CI using removed JMH_OPTS env var

The previous commit removed JMH_OPTS support from the scripts, but CI
was still passing JMH_OPTS to get fast smoke tests. This caused CI to
run with full publication-quality settings (~hours per class).

Add --smoke mode (0 forks, 1×1s warmup, 1×1s measurement) for CI smoke
tests that just verify benchmarks compile and run. Update CI workflow
to use --smoke instead of JMH_OPTS. Also update run-java-memory-benchmarks.sh
with the same --quick/--smoke/publish pattern.

commit.message:
fix(#120,#121): restrict episodic RAG to current conversation + fix accessCount update (#123)

* fix(#120,#121): restrict episodic RAG to current conversation + fix accessCount update

Bug #120 — Episodic memories from other conversations leaking in
  RagRepository.getRelevantContext() now accepts a required `conversationId`
  parameter. After fetching MessageEmbeddingEntity rows from the vector store,
  the list is filtered to only entries whose conversationId matches, so
  memories from an unrelated prior conversation (e.g. a birthday chat) can
  never be injected into the current penguin conversation even when their
  embedding distance is below the 0.4 threshold.
  ChatViewModel.sendMessage() passes `convId` as the new argument.
  All five RagRepositoryTest call-sites updated to supply conversationId.

Bug #121 — accessCount stuck at 0 on the Memory screen
  Investigation showed the SQLite column names are camelCase (accessCount,
  lastAccessedAt) — Room does NOT auto-convert to snake_case. The existing
  @Query UPDATE SQL was already syntactically correct; the root cause was that
  Room 2.7.1's @Query UPDATE path does not reliably notify InvalidationTracker,
  so observeAll() Flow never re-emitted after a stat increment.
  Fix: replace the raw @Query UPDATE batch in MemoryRepositoryImpl with a new
  @Update-annotated CoreMemoryDao.updateAllEntities() method. Room's @Update
  annotation guarantees the InvalidationTracker fires and the Memory screen
  Flow re-emits immediately with the correct accessCount.

Closes #120
Closes #121

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#120,#121): address code review — SQL-level conversationId filter + @Transaction for accessCount

- MessageEmbeddingDao: add getByRowIdsForConversation() pushing conversationId
  predicate into SQL, eliminating post-hoc in-memory filter that caused topK
  starvation when other conversations dominated the vector search results
- RagRepository: use getByRowIdsForConversation() instead of getByRowIds() +
  .filter { it.conversationId == conversationId }
- CoreMemoryDao: add @Transaction incrementAccessStatsAndNotify() wrapping
  updateAccessStatsBatch(); guarantees atomicity (no concurrent read-modify-write
  races) and InvalidationTracker notification on commit so observeAll() re-emits
- MemoryRepositoryImpl: replace updateAllEntities entity-copy approach with
  incrementAccessStatsAndNotify for correct concurrent accessCount increments
- RagRepositoryTest: update existing mocks to getByRowIdsForConversation;
  add cross-conversation isolation test to guard against topK starvation regression

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: eliminate async overhead and reduce GC pressure (~49% faster)

- Add ISyncPacketSource interface for synchronous packet consumption
- Refactor TimestampMergedReplayer to synchronous (no Channel<T>)
- Add FeedHandler.RunSyncLoop for zero-async-overhead processing
- Use ArrayPool<byte> in PcapReader (reduce 52M allocations)
- Use 1MB BufferedStream in PcapReader for batched I/O
- Track and return pooled arrays in TimestampMergedReplayer lifecycle
- Deep-copy packet data before queuing in FeedHandler (ArrayPool safety)
- Fix race condition in PrintBookSample (try/catch InvalidOperationException)
- Add [Conditional("DEBUG")] AssertValid to BookSide

Benchmark: 52M packets processed in ~30s (was ~59s before optimizations).

* Agent: Bug Fix

* fix: correct test-int-strings fixture assertion type from idempotent to transform

The test-int-strings integration fixture was misconfigured with
"assertion": "idempotent" in its gmloop.json, but contained an
expected.gml file with different content from input.gml. This caused
the fixture-runner discovery validation to throw, blocking the entire
cross-module integration test suite from running (0 of 22 tests).

Changes:
- gmloop.json: Changed assertion from "idempotent" to "transform"
- expected.gml: Updated to match current lint+format pipeline output

The previous expected.gml contained semantically incorrect output
(template interpolation syntax {fps} inside a plain string without
the $ prefix), which would not produce the same runtime behavior as
the original input. The updated expected.gml uses the correct $"..."
template string syntax.

After this fix, 12 integration tests now pass (including test-int-strings),
and 11 other pre-existing fixture mismatches are now individually visible
instead of being masked by the discovery crash.

commit.message:
fix: non-blocking partition DDL to prevent INSERT cascade stalls

Use SET LOCAL lock_timeout='0' in ensure_partition() so the CREATE TABLE
PARTITION OF command never queues an ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock when concurrent
INSERTs are active.

Previously, any busy CZDS/CertStream worker holding a ROW EXCLUSIVE lock
(e.g. a long-running trigram similarity scan) would cause the DDL to queue
indefinitely, blocking *all* subsequent INSERT batches for the parent domain
table until the DDL completed or was manually cancelled.

With lock_timeout='0':
- DDL fails immediately if lock unavailable (no queue, no cascade)
- Session is rolled back cleanly (fixes InFailedSqlTransaction on advisory unlock)
- Re-checks pg_class after rollback: if another process created the partition
  concurrently, proceeds normally
- TLD is retried on the next ingest cycle (acceptable behaviour)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(v0.5): auto query planner, metadata indexes, AVX2, fast_mode default, docs overhaul (#40)

unwrap audit) → MINOR bump per versioning policy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(search,wal): parallelize ANN search_batch + increase WAL BufWriter to 4 MB

- search_batch() uses par_iter for ANN path (1.46x speedup on batch=8, 16-core
  machine); brute-force path keeps sequential iter since each query already
  saturates the thread pool via internal par_chunks(512)
- WAL BufWriter capacity increased from default 8 KB to 4 MB, reducing system
  calls per append_batch from ~5000 to ~8 for 1536-dim entries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(readme): add brute-force vs ANN guidance + per-release comparison section

- Add 'When to use brute-force vs. ANN' section after benchmark tables:
  explains that d<=256 favors brute-force (ANN loses 22 pp recall on GloVe-200),
  while d>=1536 favors ANN (3-5x latency gain with <3% recall cost)
- Add 'Release Comparison' section with v0.4.0 as the tracking baseline;
  from v0.5.0 onward each release row will include delta vs prior version
- Update benchmark tables and perf history from fresh full run (v0.5 branch state)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Apply WAL entries to live state in order so delete-then-reinsert keeps the latest slot across flush and reopen. Preserve metadata during live compaction and commit the QA regression tests that now pass.

Provides get_eq_candidates() for fast filter candidate resolution.

* docs(benchmarks): replace interpolation comparison with power-of-two fair test

Replace the SRHT vs exact comparison table in README with results from
the new methodology: run both modes at d in {128,256,512,1024} where
next_power_of_two(d)=d, so both modes produce exactly n=d codes with
identical bit budgets and codebooks.

Key findings:
- d<=256: differences within noise (+-0.012 R@1), no consistent winner
- d=512: exact consistently +0.2-0.9 pp R@1
- d=1024 b=4: exact +3.8 pp R@1 — meaningful advantage for Haar-random QR
- d=1024 b=8: both modes at 79.6% R@1, indistinguishable near ceiling
- Speed: SRHT 1.1-17.9x faster; gap widens with d (O(d log d) vs O(d^2))

Also rewrites compare_quantizers_fair.py to use this methodology with
enforce-via-assert_power_of_two(), direct delta table (no interpolation),
and 100k synthetic Gaussian corpus at all dims.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(engine): v0.5 sprint — #29 #30 #31 #33 #34 + fast_mode dequantize fix

### #33 WAL write coalescing
Pre-build entire batch into one Vec<u8>, then one write_all + one flush
per append_batch call. Eliminates per-entry syscall overhead.

### #29 SIMD unpack_mse_indices
AVX2 variant for b=4: 16 packed bytes → 32 u16 per iteration via
_mm_unpacklo/hi_epi8 + _mm256_cvtepu8_epi16. Scalar fallback unchanged.

### #34 Cross-query parallelism in search_batch
For ANN or small-N (≤SEQ_THRESHOLD=20k) paths, par_iter() across queries.
Large-N brute-force stays serial (inner par_chunks saturates Rayon there).

### #31 Range index for metadata
BTreeMap<u64, Vec<u32>> per numeric field using IEEE 754 ordered-key
encoding (f64_to_ord). extract_range_condition() routes $gt/$gte/$lt/$lte
filters through get_range_candidates() in resolve_filter_via_index().

### #30 Auto query planner
_use_ann: Option<bool> (None=auto, True=force ANN, False=force brute).
auto_use_ann() returns true when index exists + N≥10k + delta≤20% of N.
Module-level constants: AUTO_ANN_THRESHOLD=10_000, AUTO_ANN_MAX_DELTA=20%.

### fix(quantizer): fast_mode dequantize
dequantize() short-circuits to MSE-only when fast_mode=True, preventing
a panic on the zero-length QJL slice during rerank.

Remove the large-N sequential guard in search_batch(). Rayon's work-stealing
handles nested par_iter (outer: queries) + par_chunks (inner: vectors) without
over-subscribing the thread pool. Batch throughput improves for all N; Rayon
prevents deadlock and manages core sharing automatically.

hardware variance (change does not affect the single-query search() path).

commit.message:
Make WithEndpoint update existing endpoints instead of throwing (#16039)

* Add WithHttpPort and WithHttpsPort convenience methods

Add WithHttpPort<T> and WithHttpsPort<T> extension methods on
IResourceBuilder<T> where T : IResourceWithEndpoints. These provide a
simple way to set the host port on existing HTTP/HTTPS endpoints without
needing to use the lower-level WithEndpoint callback API.

This addresses a common pain point where users want to pin a port for
local development (e.g., Vite apps, Keycloak OIDC flows, API gateways)
but the existing API either requires knowledge of endpoint internals or
throws when trying to re-add an endpoint that was auto-created.

Relates to #13807
Relates to #15873

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove redundant JS-specific tests

The WithHttpPort/WithHttpsPort methods are tested in the core
Aspire.Hosting.Tests project which covers the same code path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review feedback: use createIfNotExists: false and add negative tests

- Pass createIfNotExists: false to WithEndpoint in both WithHttpPort and
  WithHttpsPort to avoid silently creating malformed endpoints when the
  target endpoint doesn't exist.
- Add negative tests verifying no endpoint is created when http/https
  endpoints are missing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update codegen snapshots for WithHttpPort/WithHttpsPort

The new [AspireExport] methods are picked up by the polyglot codegen,
updating snapshots for TypeScript, Go, Java, Python, and Rust.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix misleading Keycloak example in WithHttpsPort docs

Keycloak creates its HTTPS endpoint lazily, so WithHttpsPort would be
a no-op when chained directly after AddKeycloak. Replace with a
container example that accurately demonstrates the API contract.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Change WithEndpoint to update existing endpoints instead of throwing

Instead of throwing when an endpoint with the same name already exists,
update the existing endpoint with any non-null parameter values. This
makes WithHttpEndpoint/WithHttpsEndpoint idempotent and removes the
need for separate WithHttpPort/WithHttpsPort convenience methods.

This directly solves the common pain point where resources like
AddViteApp auto-create an 'http' endpoint and users cannot call
WithHttpEndpoint to configure it.

Relates to #13807
Relates to #15873

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix update path: don't overwrite isProxied, avoid duplicate env annotations

- Remove unconditional isProxied override on update path. Since the
  parameter defaults to true in the public API, we can't distinguish
  'user passed true' from 'default true', so we leave it unchanged.
- Only add EnvironmentCallbackAnnotation when env is newly configured
  (TargetPortEnvironmentVariable was null) to avoid duplicates.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add tests for isProxied preservation and env annotation dedup

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix env var replacement: read TargetPortEnvironmentVariable at eval time

When the env parameter changes on an update (e.g., PORT -> NEW_PORT),
the callback now reads TargetPortEnvironmentVariable from the captured
annotation at evaluation time rather than closing over the string.
This ensures only the current env var name is set, not the stale one.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Extract ConfigureEndpointEnvironmentVariable to deduplicate env setup

Both the create and update paths now call the same helper method for
configuring the endpoint environment variable callback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add edge case tests for scheme, isProxied, and isExternal on update

- Scheme is not changed on update (by design)
- isProxied is not changed on update (can't distinguish default from explicit)
- isExternal is updated when explicitly set (nullable, so null = don't change)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Allow setting isProxied to false on endpoint update

Since the default is true, passing false is always intentional and safe
to apply. Passing true is indistinguishable from the default so it's
left as a no-op to avoid accidentally overwriting an explicit false.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Defer EndpointAnnotation creation until needed

Move the EndpointAnnotation construction after the existing-endpoint
check so we don't allocate an annotation only to throw it away on the
update path. Resolve the endpoint name inline using the same logic as
the EndpointAnnotation constructor.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Rewrite: the duck is silent — true rubber duck debugging

The original rubber duck method (The Pragmatic Programmer, 1999) is about
self-explanation to a silent, inanimate audience. The duck doesn't ask
questions, give feedback, or suggest fixes. The developer does all the
talking. Bugs reveal themselves mid-explanation.

Previous version incorrectly made the duck an interactive questioner
(Socratic method / pair programming). This rewrite corrects the approach:

- Skill: agent explains TO the silent duck, catches own discrepancies
- Agent: the duck is inanimate — exists as audience, not interrogator
- Output format preserved: Five Quacks session with structured report
- Added cognitive science references (self-explanation effect, cognitive
  offloading, psychological distance, metacognition)
- Added references section (Pragmatic Programmer, Coding Horror, Byrd 2023)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Lottie: Fix crashes on animated vector and path properties (#18282)

## Summary

Fixes crashes when rectangle size/position, path bezier data, or scalar
properties (like rounded-corner radius) are animated (`a === 1`) by
introducing shared property helpers that correctly read keyframe data
instead of casting `k` directly to static types.

## Problem

The bounding box and sprite packer code read animated properties via
direct casts like `rect.s.k as number[]`, `path.ks.k as RawBezier`, and
`shape.r.k as number`. When these properties are animated, `k` is an
array of keyframe objects, and the cast produces garbage data or
crashes.

## Solution

New shared utility module `rawPropertyHelpers.ts` with three helpers:

- **`GetInitialScalarValue`** — reads the static value (`a === 0`) or
first keyframe's value (`a === 1`) for scalar properties (e.g.,
rounded-corner radius), with a configurable default for empty keyframes
- **`GetInitialVectorValues`** — reads the static value or first
keyframe's value for vector/position properties (e.g., rectangle size
and position)
- **`GetInitialBezierData`** — reads the static bezier or first
keyframe's bezier for path shapes, with a null guard for empty keyframe
arrays

Both `boundingBox.ts` and `spritePacker.ts` now import from this shared
module instead of using direct casts. All helpers use proper keyframe
types (`RawVectorKeyframe`, `RawBezierShapeKeyframe`) instead of broad
casts for type safety.

Additionally, `GetShapesBoundingBox` now returns a safe 0×0 bounding box
when no geometry is found (e.g., empty animated keyframes), instead of
leaking `Infinity`/`NaN` values downstream.

Shapes are rasterized at their initial keyframe values — animated shape
property playback (e.g., morphing rectangles) is not supported right now
and is planned as future work.

## Tests

Added 16 unit tests covering:

**Shared helpers (10 tests):**
- Static and animated scalar value extraction
- Static and animated vector value extraction
- Static and animated bezier data extraction
- Empty keyframe edge cases for all three types
- RawPositionProperty compatibility

**Bounding box (6 tests):**
- Static rectangle at origin and non-origin positions
- Animated rectangle uses initial keyframe size
- Static path with zero tangents
- Animated path uses initial keyframe bezier
- Empty animated path keyframes returns 0×0 bounding box

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix RE Tape Echo + SpaceyDelayer silence, tame ShimmerReverb + MoogFilters

Root cause for RE Tape Echo and SpaceyDelayer silence:
Emscripten 4.x no longer auto-exports HEAPF32 on the module object.
Both worklets relied on module.HEAPF32 to create Float32Array buffer
views for copying audio data to/from WASM memory. With HEAPF32=undefined,
updateBufferViews() returned early, buffer views stayed null, and the
worklet process() silently returned without writing any output.

Fix: Rewrite both worklets with WebAssembly.instantiate interception
pattern (same as SpringReverb/MVerb/KissOfShame worklets) to capture
the WASM Memory object during module initialization.

ShimmerReverb was still too extreme:
- Tighten decay range: 0-1 → 0.3-0.75 (was 0.5-0.995)
- Scale shimmer feedback by 0.6x to prevent energy buildup
- tanhf soft clip on feedback + output (from previous commit)

MoogFilters was over-distorting:
- Add tanhf soft clip on filter output before wet/dry mix
- Apply drive as input gain before filter (was unused except Hyperion)
- Prevents resonance self-oscillation from clipping

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace lukka/run-vcpkg with manual vcpkg setup

lukka/run-vcpkg@v11 injects VCPKG_BINARY_SOURCES=clear;x-gha,readwrite
which is not supported by older vcpkg versions, causing build failure.
Switch to manual git clone + bootstrap to avoid the issue.
